signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 annual CPI inflation rose to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's March 2026 annual CPI inflation rose to 2.2% YoY (from 2.0% in February), undershooting market forecasts of 2.3-2.4% due to government fuel caps offsetting oil price surges.**[1][2][3] | NO | HIGH confidence

This data, released around April 2, 2026, confirms contained price pressures despite Middle East-driven oil shocks, with core inflation dipping to 2.2% YoY and monthly CPI at 0.
